Tests the behavior of Math.pow with a string base and negative zero exponent.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of the `Math.pow` function in JavaScript. It verifies that raising a non-numeric string, `"qeqegfhb"`, to the power of `-0` results in `1`. This test is likely used to confirm compliance with the ECMAScript specification, which states that any number raised to the power of `-0` should return `1`. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case of the `Math.pow` function.
